patch.value: pm extension --install pm-<alias> / @unbrained/pm-<alias> fails with 'Local extension source does not exist' instead of suggesting the bundled catalog alias

2026-07-04 sandbox audit #3: 'pm extension install pm-beads' falls through to local-path resolution and errors 'Local extension source does not exist: .../pm-beads'. The bundled catalog alias is 'beads' (install_command 'pm install beads --project'). Agents naturally try the package folder name (pm-beads) or npm name (@unbrained/pm-beads). Fix: before local-path fallback, match the target against bundled catalog aliases, package folder names (pm-<alias>) and npm package names (@unbrained/pm-<alias>); on near-miss emit a did-you-mean with the exact install command.
pm extension --install pm-<alias> and @unbrained/pm-<alias> resolve to the bundled catalog or fail with a suggestion listing catalog aliases; never-block recovery bundle included.

## Context
2026-07-04 sandbox audit #3: `pm extension install pm-beads` (also @unbrained/pm-beads) falls through to local-path resolution and errors 'Local extension source does not exist: .../pm-beads'. The bundled catalog alias is 'beads' (install_command `pm install beads --project`). Agents naturally try the package folder name or npm name first — never-block demands a mapping, not a dead end.

## Approach
Before the local-path fallback in the extension install resolver, match the target against: bundled catalog aliases, package folder names (pm-<alias>), and npm names (@unbrained/pm-<alias>). Exact match → install the catalog alias directly (or emit the exact install command); near-miss → did-you-mean via the existing OSA Damerau suggester.

## Definition of done
All three spellings of every bundled package either install or emit the exact catalog command; unknown names still reach the local-path error; spec covers alias, folder-name, npm-name, and near-miss paths.
